We examine whether shared audit style increases the comparability of reported
earnings within a Big 4 auditor's clientele. We posit that two clients audited by
the same firm produce more comparable earnings because the firm applies a common
set of internal rules. We note, however, that client economics may dominate.

**H1.** Two firms audited by the same Big 4 auditor exhibit greater earnings
comparability than two firms audited by different Big 4 auditors, ceteris paribus.

Consistent with our prediction, we find evidence that same-auditor pairs are more
comparable. The remainder of the paper proceeds as follows.
